text.title: Use Case Mapping Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Use Case Mapping application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every use-case MUST name ONE primary actor + ≤2 secondary actors.
rule.rationale: Multi-actor without role separation produces ambiguous responsibility.

rule.statement: Every use-case MUST state the actor's goal in active-verb form ("book a flight", "cancel subscription").
rule.rationale: Goal-less use-cases collapse into feature lists with no success criterion.

rule.statement: Main flow MUST be numbered steps (1..N) with one observable action per step.
rule.rationale: Bulleted prose obscures step boundaries and breaks QA traceability.

rule.statement: Every use-case MUST list ≥1 alternative flow (error, edge, abort) branching from a numbered main-flow step.
rule.rationale: Single-path use-cases miss the failure modes that drive 80% of bug reports.

rule.statement: Every use-case MUST end with an observable postcondition (state change, side effect).
rule.rationale: Postcondition is the only thing QA can assert on.
